text_fields.question_66404568:
My work at Microsoft has been centered on exactly the problems this role is about: owning a product roadmap for a data platform, defining what gets built and why, partnering with engineering on delivery architecture, and establishing a centralized, commercially accountable product model from scratch. I built Azure's resilience validation platform into a structured product with QA frameworks, incident communication workflows, and operational reliability standards - serving enterprise clients like Databricks and Walmart. That experience maps directly to what YipitData is asking for: someone who can own a feed portfolio end-to-end, not just maintain it.

What draws me specifically to YipitData is the combination of alternative data depth and the strategic moment the Feeds organization is in. Building a centralized product management function around high-value data feeds - transaction data, email receipt data, B2B spend - for buy-side quant and systematic investors is a genuinely interesting product challenge. It is not a solved problem, and the fact that the role carries ARR accountability alongside roadmap ownership is the kind of commercial gravity I want in my next role. The feeds and quant segment being one of the fastest-growing parts of the business makes the stakes real and the work consequential.
text_fields.question_66404570: At Microsoft I led the Resilience Automation Platform working directly with on-call engineers and partner teams as customers — gathering requirements, running customer-feedback sessions, and shipping iterations against their workflows. Earlier roles included direct customer-facing engineering across deployment, integration, and post-sales support.
text_fields.question_66411863: Yes. At Microsoft I carried commercial accountability for the resilience platform, with direct line of sight to the $14M+ in business impact delivered across the enterprise client portfolio. One decision that measurably moved commercial outcomes was pioneering Azure's first proactive rack-level testing capability. The decision required me to translate requirements from the quant research equivalent on our team - internal engineering researchers surfacing hardware failure patterns - into a new drill program. I shipped it in four months with a 94% recovery rate, and it surfaced critical hardware defects that would otherwise have reached clients as incidents. That program became a key retention and expansion argument for enterprise accounts, because it demonstrated proactive risk management rather than reactive remediation. Separately, at Pro Painters I conducted profitability analysis across 1,000+ project records, identified margin leaks, and built a framework for packaging and competitive positioning that directly informed P&L decisions - reducing customer acquisition cost by 13% and lifting conversion rates by 2.7%. Both examples reflect the same instinct: use data to make a product decision that has a clear commercial downstream.
text_fields.question_66404571: I have learned a lot at Microsoft and I am proud of what the resilience platform became, but I am at a point where I want to own a product that is closer to the commercial core of the business - where my roadmap decisions have a direct and visible line to revenue outcomes and client retention. The infrastructure work at Microsoft is meaningful, but the path to that kind of accountability is long and indirect there. YipitData's model - where the PM shares ARR accountability alongside the Product Specialist and has a real P&L stake in the feeds they own - is exactly the environment I am looking to move into. I also want to work on data products where the end users are sophisticated and demanding, like buy-side quant investors, because that sharpens the product thinking in ways that enterprise infrastructure does not. The timing feels right: I have built the cross-functional and operational foundations, and I want to apply them somewhere the commercial feedback loop is tighter.
text_fields.question_66404572: First, alternative data domain expertise - specifically understanding how buy-side quant and systematic investors use transaction data, email receipt data, and B2B spend data in signal testing, backtesting, and alpha generation workflows. I have strong data product instincts but I want to develop deep fluency in the specific use cases and quality standards this end market demands. Second, commercial product ownership - I have had accountability tied to business impact metrics, but I want to sharpen the specific muscles around pricing, packaging, competitive positioning, and managing a product P&L in a direct, structured way. YipitData's model makes that real in a way most PM roles do not. Third, third-party data vendor management - I have worked on data partnerships at the infrastructure level but I want to develop the skill of evaluating and managing external data vendor relationships, understanding licensing constraints and coverage tradeoffs, and using that to make smarter product decisions upstream.

dropdowns.label: I have submitted a PDF version of my resume.
Over 5 years of Product Management experience, but NOT in data-intensive products or B2B SaaS.

needs_review_dropdowns.label: Open to remote, hybrid, or onsite
needs_review_dropdowns.question: I acknowledge that for remote-based US roles, depending upon where the remote work is performed, INCOME COULD BE SUBJECT TO NYS TAX WITHHOLDINGS.

cover_overrides.label: why are you interested in this role at yipitdata? what about this job opportunity compels you?

have you had direct accountability for revenue metrics (e.g., arr, p&l, retention) for a product you managed? if so, give a brief example of a product decision you made that measurably impacted commercial outcomes.
why are you looking for new opportunities right now?
please rank the top 3 skill sets that you'd like to further develop at yipitdata.
please explain any employment gaps in your work history. if none, say “n/a.”

args.comment: Stage the file into the visually-hidden #resume input via CDP setInputFiles. Filestack will commit it on the next Attach click. Files must be under /tmp/openclaw/uploads/.
